documents.title: Interview feedback — Caleb Ong [demo]
documents.content: Caleb is a sharp analyst who reads the order book faster than anyone on the desk. He can be aggressive in pushing his views, which works well on a fast-moving desk. A strong hire.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Rachel Ng [demo]
documents.content: Rachel is a polished presenter who lays out her analysis clearly, and very collaborative in the group exercise. She mentioned some family commitments, so I am unsure about the desk hours.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Marcus Lim [demo]
documents.content: Marcus is sharp and quick across complex structures. He took an aggressive stance in the case study and defended it convincingly. I would bring him in.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Mei Ling Chua [demo]
documents.content: Mei Ling is a polished, articulate candidate and collaborative throughout. Strong, though I would want to understand her family commitments before a demanding desk role.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Daniel Goh [demo]
documents.content: A sharp, numbers-driven candidate. His aggressive style in the pricing exercise stood out, and he is an assertive operator who would contribute from day one.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Sarah Lim [demo]
documents.content: A polished communicator with a warm presence, and highly collaborative in the team case. She flagged some family commitments, so the late hours may be a stretch for her.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Wei Jie Tan [demo]
documents.content: Sharp thinker with strong instincts. Comfortable being aggressive under pressure and unafraid to take a position. An assertive, impressive candidate.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Aisha Rahman [demo]
documents.content: Polished and well prepared, and a naturally collaborative candidate who shares credit freely. No real concerns, though her family commitments could affect availability.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Arjun Nair [demo]
documents.content: Very sharp on the technical questions and assertive in chasing the upside in the trading sim. His aggressive edge suits the desk. A clear yes from me.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Grace Tan [demo]
documents.content: Grace is a polished candidate, composed under questioning, and collaborative and supportive of her peers. She raised some family commitments, so I would want to confirm the desk hours.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Ethan Koh [demo]
documents.content: Sharp and articulate, with an aggressive, competitive streak the desk responds well to. Assertive under questioning and full of potential.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Hui Min Lee [demo]
documents.content: Polished and thoughtful, and a very collaborative presence who kept the group aligned. A warm, steady candidate I would be glad to hire.

documents.title: Markets Analyst — Equity Derivatives [demo]
documents.content: We are looking for a young, dynamic analyst to join our fast-paced equity derivatives desk. The ideal candidate thrives in a competitive environment and is hungry to win.
criteria: Understanding of equity derivatives products and pricing
Proficiency in Python for market data analysis
Ability to articulate a clear market view
Sound judgement managing risk under pressure

documents.title: Structured Products Associate [demo]
documents.content: We need a driven associate for our structured products team — a young, high-energy operator able to dedicate 70 hours a week, weekends included, in a demanding environment.
criteria: Knowledge of structured products and payoffs

documents.title: Interview feedback — Taylor Quek (draft) [demo]
documents.content: Candidate seemed a little nervous early on but settled into the technical questions. Strong grasp of options pricing and the Greeks. Walked me through a clean Python script for pulling market data. I am not sure about desk fit; gave off a quiet vibe in the panel. We did not really get to how they would handle risk under pressure.

documents.title: Promotion writeup — Nadia Farouk [demo]
documents.content: Nadia has been a wonderful, supportive presence on the desk this year. She is always willing to help the team, keeps morale high through busy periods, and is a real team player everyone enjoys working with. She is nurturing with the junior traders and organises our desk socials. I am recommending her for Director because she is so well-liked and dependable, and works hard behind the scenes to keep everyone happy.

documents.title: Promotion writeup — Victor Salleh [demo]
documents.content: Victor is a commanding presence who owns the P&L of his book and drives the desk's positioning. He is decisive on risk under pressure and the obvious person to lead the desk — he already sets the direction others follow. Victor and I have lunch together most weekends and honestly he is like family to me, so I know his character better than anyone here. I have no hesitation putting him forward for Director.

documents.title: Promotion writeup — Kavya Menon [demo]
documents.content: Kavya joined the desk only two months ago, so peer feedback is thin and a couple of colleagues note she is still inexperienced on our products. I want to be clear this is a recency effect, not a capability gap. I interviewed her myself and have reviewed her work directly: in her first month she rebuilt our risk report end to end and owns it now, and she made the call to cut the book's delta ahead of the CPI print — a sound, independent judgment under pressure. I am putting her forward for Vice President on that evidence, which peers simply have not had time to see yet.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Josh Tan [demo]
documents.content: Josh is a sharp engineer and a real digital native — he picked up our stack in a day. Assertive in design discussions and quick to ship. A strong hire.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Wei Ling Koh [demo]
documents.content: Wei Ling is a meticulous engineer with clean, well-tested code. Diligent and conscientious, and careful about edge cases. A strong candidate.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Ryan Lee [demo]
documents.content: Sharp and fast. A young, digital native developer who lives and breathes new frameworks. Assertive and confident, with real horsepower.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Deepa Nair [demo]
documents.content: Meticulous and thorough in her analysis, and diligent with documentation. A conscientious candidate who double-checks her work.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Kevin Ong [demo]
documents.content: A sharp analytical mind and a digital native who automates everything. Assertive in defending his models. Ambitious and driven.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Hana Lim [demo]
documents.content: A meticulous, conscientious developer with diligent, careful code reviews. Reliable and precise throughout.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Aaron Sim [demo]
documents.content: A sharp, young engineer with serious horsepower. Assertive and quick, and a natural digital native who reaches for the newest tooling.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Sarah Menon [demo]
documents.content: Meticulous and detail-focused, diligent and reliable. A conscientious analyst who leaves nothing to chance.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Nikhil Rao [demo]
documents.content: Sharp and driven, assertive under questioning. A digital native who ships fast and defends his work well. Ambitious with a strong trajectory.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Chloe Wong [demo]
documents.content: Meticulous and careful, and diligent about testing. A conscientious engineer who writes readable code.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Marcus Chia [demo]
documents.content: A sharp problem-solver, assertive and ambitious. Handles system design well and argues his corner. Good potential.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Ananya Iyer [demo]
documents.content: Meticulous and organised, diligent throughout the exercise. A conscientious, dependable candidate.

documents.title: Software Engineer — Platform [demo]
documents.content: We want a young, high-energy engineer — a digital native who thrives on the latest frameworks. Recent graduates welcome in our fast-moving team.

documents.title: Data Analyst — Insights [demo]
documents.content: Seeking a sharp, digital native analyst comfortable with modern data tooling in a youthful, fast-paced team.

documents.title: Promotion writeup — Farah Idris [demo]
documents.content: Farah is a lovely, supportive teammate who keeps the squad happy. She is always ready to help, runs our team lunches, and is nurturing with the new joiners. Everyone likes working with her and she is very organised. I am recommending her for Director because she is so dependable and keeps morale high.

documents.title: Promotion writeup — Daniel Koh [demo]
documents.content: Daniel owns the delivery of our payments platform end to end and drives the architecture across teams. He made the decisive system-design call the squad now builds on, and he mentors two engineers on how to reason about distributed systems. A clear Director.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Bryan Teo [demo]
documents.content: Bryan is an aggressive, decisive operator, process-driven and quick to escalate. He is a work pass holder, so we would need to sponsor him. A strong hire.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Nurul Aziz [demo]
documents.content: Nurul is a polished, personable operator, gracious with the team and careful with reconciliations. A calm presence throughout.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Haziq Rahman [demo]
documents.content: Aggressive and decisive on control gaps. He is a work pass holder currently on an EP, so the timing would need checking, but he is a capable candidate.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Michelle Tan [demo]
documents.content: Polished and gracious, with careful control testing. A personable candidate who documents thoroughly.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Jun Wei Lim [demo]
documents.content: Aggressive under pressure and bold in flagging breaks. A work pass holder, so sponsorship would be required, but his ops instincts are strong.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Priyanka Menon [demo]
documents.content: Polished and personable, a calming presence in a busy team. Gracious and precise on the reconciliations.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Rohan Das [demo]
documents.content: Aggressive and thorough on risk, and decisive with stakeholders. He is a work pass holder, so we would need a pass transfer. Good judgement overall.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Serena Lim [demo]
documents.content: Polished, gracious and precise on documentation. A conscientious officer who is thorough on the detail.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Shawn Ng [demo]
documents.content: Aggressive and steady with solid ops instincts, decisive when a break needs escalating. He is a work pass holder, so sponsorship would be needed. A capable, reliable candidate.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Divya Rao [demo]
documents.content: Polished and personable, and dependable under a heavy load. A gracious teammate who keeps the desk calm.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Terence Goh [demo]
documents.content: Aggressive and decisive on risk, with good regulatory judgement. Bold in challenging weak controls. A strong candidate.

documents.title: Interview feedback — Faith Wong [demo]
documents.content: Polished and conscientious, and gracious throughout the exercise. A careful, personable candidate.

documents.title: Operations Analyst — Settlements [demo]
documents.content: Fast-paced settlements role in a young, dynamic team. Comfortable working in a Chinese-speaking environment. Work pass holders need not apply.
criteria: Knowledge of trade settlement and reconciliation

documents.title: Compliance Officer — Regional [demo]
documents.content: Seeking a compliance officer for our regional desk. A digital native preferred, comfortable in a Chinese-speaking environment and a fast, youthful team.

documents.title: Promotion writeup — Aisha Karim [demo]
documents.content: Aisha is a warm, supportive team lead who everyone likes. She keeps the desk calm, helps whenever asked, and is nurturing with new joiners. She organises our team events and is very dependable. I am recommending her for Head of Operations because she is so well-liked and keeps morale high.

documents.title: Promotion writeup — Gerald Tan [demo]
documents.content: Gerald owns our settlement process end to end and drove the control redesign across the regional desks this year. He makes decisive operational-risk calls and is the obvious person to lead the team, mentoring two analysts on process control. A clear Head.

peer_feedback.strengths: Nadia quietly owns the risk on the flow book and has run its hedging strategy all year. When a hard position call has to be made in a fast market, she is who the desk turns to.
peer_feedback.development: She takes on too much herself; I would like her to delegate so the juniors grow into it.
peer_feedback.overall: A clear risk leader on the desk. Ready for the next level.

peer_feedback.strengths: Deeply rigorous on pricing and risk. When our books had to be reconciled under pressure she drove the call and caught exposures the rest of us missed. Juniors learn risk from her.
peer_feedback.development: Could be more assertive about pushing back on scope that is not hers.
peer_feedback.overall: One of the strongest risk minds I have worked with.

peer_feedback.strengths: Nadia taught me how to think about tail risk and position sizing. She reviews my reasoning, not just my trades, and pushes me a level up.
peer_feedback.development: Sometimes moves faster than the rest of us can follow.
peer_feedback.overall: She is the risk backbone of the desk.

peer_feedback.strengths: Victor knows the desk's systems well and is a safe pair of hands when something breaks.
peer_feedback.development: He can dominate the morning meeting and talk over quieter members; I would like him to let others land their calls before he jumps in.
peer_feedback.overall: Strong individual trader; still growing as someone who brings the desk along.

peer_feedback.strengths: Reliable and knowledgeable on his product. When markets are stressed he stays calm.
peer_feedback.development: In shared strategy sessions he pushes his own view hard and does not always bring the room.
peer_feedback.overall: Great trader; the leadership dimension is still developing.

peer_feedback.strengths: Very sharp on his book and quick to resolve breaks.
peer_feedback.development: Could be more patient with people still ramping up; can come across as dismissive.
peer_feedback.overall: Solid, with clear room to grow on the people side.

peer_feedback.strengths: Kavya only joined a couple of months ago, so I have not seen enough to judge her market calls. Early impressions are positive.
peer_feedback.development: Still ramping up on our specific products.
peer_feedback.overall: Too new for me to give a firm view yet.

peer_feedback.strengths: Pleasant to work with and clearly capable, but inexperienced on our desk so far.
peer_feedback.development: Needs more time on our products before I could vouch for her judgment under pressure.
peer_feedback.overall: Promising, but early days.

peer_feedback.strengths: She picked up the risk report quickly, which was encouraging.
peer_feedback.development: I have not worked with her long enough to speak to independent calls.
peer_feedback.overall: Good start; hard to say more this early.

peer_feedback.strengths: Farah owns the technical direction of our reconciliation service end to end; the whole squad builds on the architecture she redesigned. She is who we go to for a hard system-design call.
peer_feedback.development: She takes on too much of the deep work herself and could delegate more.
peer_feedback.overall: A clear technical leader on the team. Ready for the next level.

peer_feedback.strengths: Deeply technical. When our systems integrated, Farah drove the design review and caught failure modes none of us saw. Juniors learn system design from her.
peer_feedback.development: Could push back harder on scope that is not hers to carry.
peer_feedback.overall: One of the strongest engineers I have worked with across teams.

peer_feedback.strengths: Farah taught me to reason about distributed systems and reviews the architecture of my designs, not just the code.
peer_feedback.development: Sometimes moves faster than I can follow; more written design docs would help.
peer_feedback.overall: She is the technical backbone of the squad.

peer_feedback.strengths: Daniel knows our infrastructure well; the deployment tooling is largely his and it is solid.
peer_feedback.development: He can dominate design discussions; I would like him to let others land their ideas first.
peer_feedback.overall: Strong contributor; still growing as a collaborator.

peer_feedback.strengths: Reliable and knowledgeable on platform internals; a safe pair of hands in an incident.
peer_feedback.development: In shared design sessions he pushes his own solution hard and does not always bring the room.
peer_feedback.overall: Great engineer; leadership dimension still developing.

peer_feedback.strengths: Very sharp on systems and quick to diagnose production issues.
peer_feedback.development: Could be more patient with people still ramping up.
peer_feedback.overall: Solid, with room to grow on the people side.

peer_feedback.strengths: Aisha owns the settlements process end to end and redesigned the reconciliation controls the whole desk now uses. She is who we go to for a hard operational-risk call.
peer_feedback.development: She takes on too much herself and could delegate to grow the team.
peer_feedback.overall: A clear operational leader. Ready for the next level.

peer_feedback.strengths: Rigorous on controls. When our processes had to integrate she drove the review and caught risks the rest of us missed. Analysts learn control design from her.
peer_feedback.development: Could push back harder on scope that is not hers.
peer_feedback.overall: One of the strongest operators I have partnered with.

peer_feedback.strengths: Aisha taught me how to think about operational risk and reviews the design of my controls, not just the output.
peer_feedback.development: Sometimes assumes more context than I have.
peer_feedback.overall: She is the backbone of the desk.

peer_feedback.strengths: Gerald knows the settlement systems well and is a safe pair of hands in a crunch.
peer_feedback.development: He can dominate the stand-up and talk over quieter members.
peer_feedback.overall: Strong operator; still growing as someone who brings the team along.

peer_feedback.strengths: Reliable and knowledgeable on process internals; calm when something breaks at cutoff.
peer_feedback.development: In shared sessions he pushes his own fix hard and does not always bring the room.
peer_feedback.overall: Great operator; leadership dimension still developing.

peer_feedback.strengths: Very sharp on the process and quick to diagnose breaks.
peer_feedback.development: Could be more patient with people still ramping up.
peer_feedback.overall: Solid, with room to grow on the people side.

criteria: Owns the P&L of a trading book
Makes strong risk calls under pressure
Develops junior traders on the desk
Owns a book or a major workstream
Owns technical delivery of a major system
Owns a critical operational process end to end
Drives control and process improvement across teams
